verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass TM protein with a large extracellular ectodomain that reaches the outer leaflet of the sperm plasma membrane following the acrosome reaction. However, its surface display is restricted to spermatozoa (germline/reproductive lineage) and depends on a specific activation state (acrosome reaction-triggered translocation from acrosomal membranes). The protein has its own TM anchor and achieves documented surface accessibility, but only in a narrow specialized cell type and state — meeting the definition of tissue_restricted_surface with an activation-state component.
